reviews.review_title: Best soccer game on the Quest 3, but....
reviews.review_description:
This game needs lots of improving, delete the games room and the bad weather effects and free up room for actual football stuff and performance.
I give it a 5 stars because you are a small team that have actually created a much needed soccer game on the Quest 3, but I would really like to see many improvements.

Search Youtube for Gotcha VR, I have just uploaded my Year later Review, also I've added a full game with the different camera angles for you guys to see them and how they look.

reviews.review_title: Has potential but the controls are awful
reviews.review_description:
Firstly I will simply stare that this game isn't for me.

The wide variety of options for camera angles, ways to play and customisation what stops me giving this game 1 star. You get options between a Football Manager style sim, Fifa-like game or even an imersive first person and I love the potential that each of these game plays off. Sadly all of them fall short of what they could be. 

The graphics are good.

Controls are awful. There is very little intuitive about them and what you expect the way to be pass, shoot or tackle, isn't. Players get fatigued too quickly, tackling is too complicated and the whole game just feels like a beta test work in progress.

As I said, it's not for me and you might pick up the controls easier than me so I would say given it a go but don't go over the two hour return window.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the honest feedback and for giving the game a try.

That is fair. Even though we put a lot of effort into making the controls as intuitive as possible, this is still a game where every control style needs a bit of learning time.

The good news is that the Standard control mode is meant to be the easiest starting point: passing is done with A, and almost everything else uses the trigger. It is usually the best option for getting comfortable with the game before trying the more advanced control styles.

We really appreciate you taking the time to explain your experience. Feedback like this helps us keep improving Motion Soccer.

reviews.review_description: the game is good and has a very good kick to it but the ai isn't the best and in referee mode the whistle it's kinda messed up sometimes when you blow it and uh when you call fouls it doesn't really work the best and stuff but it is easy fixes but i did realize that before they started focusing on the referee updates that the whistle and stuff was doing fine so i don't know what happened

developer_response.body:
You’re right that the AI and referee mode still need work. We’re actively improving those systems, but for a small indie team like us, positive reviews are extremely important. They help the game get more visibility, more players, and give us the chance to keep working on updates.

If you enjoy the game overall and believe in its potential, it would really help us a lot if you could consider changing your review to 5 stars. We’ll keep working hard to make the referee mode and AI better with every update.

reviews.review_description: This game would be super fun if these were fixed. The first thing is whenever I play in first person mode it blinks black every second and then blinks back to normal, the only mode that I can play that won’t do that is referee mode and that also barley works, whenever I call a free kick it has me mark where is should be kicked and when I am done it just stays there and doesn’t load

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback. The black blinking issue usually happens when the voice command files were not downloaded correctly. You can fix it by reinstalling the game, or you can go to Options and disable microphone/voice control.

About the free kick: first you need to mark the exact point where you want the free kick to be taken from, and then draw a line where you want the wall to stand. After doing both steps, the play should continue.

reviews.review_description:
Since getting a quest I’ve been longing for a decent footie game and this one has plenty of potential.  

It can clearly be seen the devs have already put a lot of love and hard work into this game. 

Ideally I would love to have an isometric footie game that I could play as though it was on my living room floor like a Subuteo pitch. 

Unfortunately the last two attempts at playing this have been frustrating. The controls are far from intuitive and from what I have aeen ao far this would play so much better if we could use a console controller and have the same controls as you would find in PES or Fifa
I get the feeling they are trying to cover too many bases by having so many different modes and not really getting any of the them perfect. 

To be fair I havnt given it enough time but the barrier to entry seems so high that im not sure it wiil see the time and will drop to one star at some point if they dont sort out the controls. Trying to play a match with a friend was also equally frustrating. 

So much potential and almost there so lets hope they sort the issues out

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback. We completely understand your point about the learning curve. Even though we have tried to simplify the controls, Motion Soccer is still a football game, and it usually takes a few matches before everything starts to feel natural.

Just to clarify: you can already play in mixed reality with an Xbox-style controller, using controls that are closer to a traditional football game, but simplified to make the learning curve smoother. You can also choose the control style and gameplay mode that best suits you in the options menu.

That said, we take this feedback very seriously. We will keep improving the tutorials, the first-time experience and the overall control feel to make the game easier to understand and less frustrating at the beginning.

reviews.review_description: I play mostly as a referee, I used to play the non pro version first, then when I played the pro version I've noticed that once I open the referee tablet I get a massive frame drop, and performance issue, will only be solved once the tablet is closed, is that normal?

developer_response.body:
That performance drop when opening the referee tablet is not normal. Please try going to Options and disabling the microphone voice commands option, as this can sometimes affect performance when the tablet is open.

If the issue continues, please also try reinstalling the game, as that may fix corrupted or outdated local files. 
Thanks again for letting us know!

reviews.review_description: The best part of this game is playing like a ref, the other parts are an absolute piece of trash

developer_response.body:
We're really glad to hear that you enjoyed the referee mode, because it's one of the most unique parts of Motion Soccer and we're putting a lot of care into expanding and improving it.

It would help us a lot if you could tell us which parts felt the worst to you, or what changes you think would make the game better, because specific feedback like that is what helps us improve the most. And if you truly enjoyed the referee mode, we would really appreciate it if you could reconsider your current star rating. For a small project like ours, every review has a huge impact and helps us keep improving Motion Soccer.

reviews.review_description: made my meta lag and crash no maybe not work f this game i hate it the game is a piece of sh**

developer_response.body: please. Restart your device and try again. If the problem persist write me support@ivanovichgames.com

reviews.review_description: this game is a diamond in the rough of vr soccer games. this game has GENERATIONAL potential, the only problems i have are the ball physics, and for some reason when im with the ball, i gradually slow down on the ball and i get tackled. this game has potential to be one of the greatest vr games of all time.

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for the review — calling it a “diamond in the rough” with generational potential honestly means a lot to us 🙌

You’re absolutely right about the two points you mention. Ball physics are actively being improved, and the slowdown while dribbling is linked to the stamina system, which we’re still tuning to make it feel better and more responsive.

Feedback like yours helps us focus on exactly the right things. We’ll keep pushing.

reviews.review_description: Keep them up
Good game a little buggy but overall fun like fifa but in vr
One thing i hope to see in future updates is a ref career

developer_response.body:
Really glad you’re enjoying the game!

A referee career is exactly the kind of feature we want to keep fighting for, and getting the game’s score as close as possible to 5 stars can genuinely help us get there. We’re a very small indie team, and every 5-star review gives the game more strength, more visibility and more chances to keep growing with bigger updates.

So if you’re having fun with the game and want to help us push things like a real referee career mode, changing your review to 5 stars would mean a lot.

Thanks for the support!

reviews.review_description: Nice game. Bit frustrated with controls

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for the review and for trying the game.

If the controls feel frustrating, please have a look at the Options menu, because the game offers several very different control styles. Classic uses buttons in a more traditional way, Laser lets you aim with the laser, and the Expert modes rely much more on physical movement.

Sometimes the difference between enjoying the controls and struggling with them is simply finding the style that suits you best. And if you want to share more specific feedback, feel free to join us on Discord — it really helps us keep improving the game.

reviews.review_description: Best game but a few sugestions 1:ad throphy lifting and allow you to still lift it

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot! Really glad you’re enjoying the game.

And yes, that’s actually on our roadmap. We definitely want to improve the trophy celebration and allow you to keep lifting and interacting with the trophy in a more natural way. Thanks for the suggestion!

reviews.review_description: This game if like fifa but in vr. The reference mode is my fav

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for your review and for recommending the game!

We’re really happy to hear you’re enjoying it, especially Referee Mode. And the best part is that we’re just getting started: over the next few days, Referee Mode will begin to grow with new modes, progression, Corrupt Referee, and more surprises.

As a very small indie team, we depend heavily on store visibility to keep improving the game and continue adding content. And that visibility mainly comes from 5-star reviews.

So if you’re having a great time with the game and feel it deserves it, updating your review to 5 stars would help us a lot more than you can imagine. Thank you again for playing and supporting Motion Soccer!

reviews.review_description: Well it is really good

reviews.review_title: It won't let me play career mode😭
reviews.review_description: It won't let me play Career mode when I try playing a match it doesn't let me.😢😔

developer_response.body:
Hi! We’ve just released version 1.9.7 which fixes the issue that prevented the first Career Mode match from starting. It should work correctly now.

If you have a moment to try it again, we’d love to hear if everything is working properly for you now. And of course, if you feel the update improves your experience, you’re always welcome to update your review. Thanks again for helping us improve the game!

reviews.review_description: Fun game to mess around in
Ref mode is cool

This game give us the closest experience to real life. I can’t believe how good this game is. Way to go developers. I can’t wait how much better it will be in the near future. Everyone buy this game and support the developer team.

reviews.review_title: Getting better but not there yet
reviews.review_description:
UPDATE: 1/11/26. Not sure if you updated, but I went to start the game, it closed out, I restarted and lost my in game progress (wasn't much) and settings. Other things, way too much falling and flopping by the players. The goalkeeper keeps coming way out of his net...he runs to the corner to block.  Also, adding ability to change ball color and make it more "solid" looking would be nice. It's too light gray and hard to see. Previously it was brown. Does it depend on the team? I'll rate it 4 with the current effort by the devs and hope it gets better.
_____________________________

Game is getting better since my last review, however, the game still needs tweaked.  The coach only mode is great, but why can't we coach from the aerial/blimp view of the game? There should be an option ore-game to select this mode (coach only, etc).  It's clunky to only be able to coach from sidelines the accidently hit a button and now you're a player.
Also, the commentary needs updated. It sounds like they are in a huge empty tunnel. The commentary shouldn't have echo to it.  It should match the atmosphere.

Still some bizarre AI decisions happening by the players.

The main menu needs to include the ability to be coach only and view from any angle.

Game is progressing but not there yet.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the detailed update and feedback.

Regarding the ball: the ball model is chosen randomly in every match. We have many different models, all inspired by official footballs, and they are generally designed to be bright and eye-catching. That said, we’ll review them again in case any specific one doesn’t read well visually.

Losing progress is something that genuinely surprises us. The game uses an automatic save system with local backup and Meta cloud sync. We haven’t seen other reports like this so far, but we’ll stay alert in case more players experience something similar.

About the AI: it’s being improved continuously, but in the end it also depends a lot on how each match develops. It’s true that goalkeepers sometimes take risky decisions, but that also happens in real football—some days they make mistakes, other days they have memorable performances.

Finally, your idea about defining the type of experience you want has been very valuable. We’re already preparing an update that will let players clearly specify how they want to play. One of those options will be “Coach only”, and the game will adapt itself to that role. We believe this will help each player get the experience they’re looking for, especially since there’s currently a lot of depth hidden inside the options that some players may never discover.

Thanks again for the constructive feedback—it’s genuinely helping shape the game.

reviews.review_description: as a huge soccer fan/player, this is one of the coolest soccer games i have played since VRFS, but it is not a 5 star games cuz it has some bugs, and pls make it where whenever you are far from the goal and it makes you the goalie, it slows the times and you down so it is basically a free goal from the other team pls make it where it is still slowed down but you are not. but still a great game for soccer fans

developer_response.body:
Hi again, just a quick follow-up.

Thanks once more for the detailed feedback and the kind words — especially coming from a soccer fan, that really means a lot. The goalkeeper slow-motion situation you described is a very valid point, and it’s something we’ve been actively refining to avoid those “free goal” moments while still keeping reactions readable and fair.

A lot of polishing and stability work has gone into the game since then, especially around edge cases like this. If you ever feel like revisiting the game in its current state, we’d genuinely love to hear how it feels now — and of course, updating the review is always appreciated if your experience improves.

Thanks again for the support and for helping us fine-tune the game.

reviews.review_description: Mint
This game hella tuff boiiii game deserves way more recognition aswell!

Ive had this for a while now and every match i play i use expert mode
please make ball less sensitive because when i slightly hit it it either goes powershot or it doesnt react at all

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review! Expert is intentionally very sensitive and unforgiving, since it’s designed for maximum control and realism — but you’re right that the ball response can feel too extreme in some situations (either overpowered or not reacting enough).

We’re actively tuning ball sensitivity and contact thresholds, especially for Expert, to make the response more consistent without losing precision.

If you’re enjoying the game and believe in where it’s heading, updating the rating to 5★ would really help support ongoing development. Feedback like yours is exactly what helps us fine-tune systems like this and push the game further.

reviews.review_description: I genuinely can't shoot the ball without it going to mars DON'T PLAY!!!

developer_response.body:
Motion Soccer has different control modes, so shooting depends on how you play:

🎮 Standard + automatic aiming:
Press A to pass and trigger to shoot. The game helps with aiming, so the ball should definitely not “go to Mars”.

🔦 Standard + laser aiming:
The ball goes where you aim with the laser. If you aim high, the shot goes high.

⚽ Expert control:
Shooting is fully manual, so it takes practice, just like real football.

Please check your control mode first. The game can be played in a very simple way, but Expert mode is meant to be more demanding. Need help? [support@ivanovichgames.com](mailto:support@ivanovichgames.com) or our Discord.

reviews.review_description: it best for me training my skill

I have a question. I have enabled the swing arm acceleration in the control menu, but it has no effect in the game. The L3 button also doesn't work to continuously accelerate; it just performs a dribbling move

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the question, happy to clarify 👍 The option you’re referring to allows your character to run by using an arm-swing gesture. You need to mimic a natural running motion, raising one arm while lowering the other and then switching, just like when you run in real life. This gesture makes your player run in the game, but it won’t let you go faster than the player’s maximum speed.

As for L3, when you have the ball it triggers a sprint dribble, and when you don’t have the ball it simply activates sprint. The effect is more noticeable with some players than others and also depends on how tired the player is at that moment.

reviews.review_description:
This game is getting better, but shoots itself in the foot by trying to be 3 games! A MR third-person tabletop game, a first-person manager game, or an in person, full motion football simulator.

The best is the MR desktop version, but there's no focus on just making that the game. A desktop MR football game, like Demeo, could work, but this is still trying to do too much. Also, first-person football in VR is nausea inducing.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for taking the time to share such detailed feedback — it’s appreciated.

Our goal isn’t to be three different games, but to offer multiple ways to experience football in VR, so players can choose what fits them best. Some enjoy the MR tabletop / coach-style view, others prefer first-person immersion, and many switch between modes depending on the situation.

That said, you’re absolutely right that the MR desktop-style mode is very appealing, and we’re continuing to refine and improve it. At the same time, first-person play is entirely optional — if it causes discomfort, you can fully enjoy the game without using that perspective.

VR comfort is something we take very seriously, and we keep adjusting movement, stamina, camera behavior and difficulty to make the experience smoother for as many players as possible.

Thanks again for the honest feedback — it helps us keep improving and clarifying the direction of the game.

reviews.review_description: The game is good and has good graphics and features. The modes are good especially the referee mode. The referee mode is good but when its not clear if the ball is over the line that the keeper and other players kick it away, that would make it more realistic. The other nodes are great and i would recomend it if you are a fan of football games

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for the positive feedback, we really appreciate it 👍  
We’re very glad you’re enjoying the game, especially the referee mode.

Just to be transparent: 4-star reviews, even when they’re positive, do lower the overall rating, which has a real impact on visibility for a small indie team like us. That said, we truly value your feedback and we’re committed to keep improving the game with the help of players like you.

Thanks again for supporting the game and taking the time to share your thoughts.

reviews.review_description: The game wont even work properly

developer_response.body:
Hi troopah05, Could you tell us what exactly isn’t working properly? Is the game not launching, crashing, performance issues, controls…? If you share a bit more detail, we can look into it right away and try to help you fix it.

You can also contact us directly at support@ivanovichgames.com
 so we can assist you faster. We’re constantly updating the game and we really appreciate the feedback — especially when something isn’t working as it should.

reviews.review_description: Feels like you're really in the game! I love all the different game modes. Well done!

Wow this game is amazing. Currently trying it out on Horizon+. Didn't realize there was a free and paid version. Never thought I'd play a soccer game again. The mechanics, camera views amd gameplay style is unique and interesting. I can play in so many different ways. While some mechanics seem a little clunky overall a really fun game!
This game is ok when the other team gets fouled they take forever to kick it so annoying go fix your game

developer_response.body:
Thanks for pointing that out — you’re right, those delays were frustrating and broke the pace of the match.

We’ve since fixed the issue, and free kicks and goal kicks are now taken much faster in the current version.

Appreciate you calling it out.

reviews.review_description: After a couple of games it no longer works correctly. When I look at the pitch in the center whilst accessing the large board I’m too short and can’t aim the controller at the board to select an option.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for reporting this and sorry for the frustration. This usually happens when the player height or position gets offset. Pressing the **Y button** will instantly recenter you at the correct height and position in any situation.

If it still happens after that, restarting the Meta Quest headset should fully resolve it.

We’re also keeping an eye on this to make the recentering even more robust in future updates. Thanks for taking the time to share the issue.

reviews.review_description:
Nothing like it is described as, referee is the only good part, 


I change my mind, this game is genuinely fun and can be buggy for me at times. I was genuinely pissed but ibr I’m just crap at the game 😂 it’s got massive potential to go amazingly well and I enjoy playing it all the time. 5/5 stars!!

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for giving it another chance and for updating your review.

We really appreciate the honesty, and we’re glad you ended up enjoying the game. And yes, it can definitely be challenging at first, so if you want a smoother start, remember you can lower the difficulty in the settings. You’ll probably enjoy it even more while you get used to the gameplay.

Thanks again for the support. Comments like this really motivate us to keep improving the game.

reviews.review_description: Brilliant game

I never usually write reviews but this game has really shocked me it’s so unique to other vr football games and plays really well the 3 styles work so well it doesn’t matter what type of gameplay you like if you enjoy football (soccer) chances are you will love this game it’s been great and I love how the developers listen so much to community feedback.  

Pros:
3 play styles.
Pretty much any team you can think of.
It’s cheap asf
Multiplayer (you can team with or against people)
Great ai
(There’s more just hard to list)

Cons: 
.animations could be better but they work
.I feel as if the ball raises a bit easy when shooting in expert mode
.as soon as the keeper is in range of the ball in the box you can’t really do anything about it like you can’t chip dribble or anything it’s like a barrier (it’s really hard to explain but play the free version to figure out what I mean its playable but I feel like I’ve missed a couple goals because of this.)

Overall amazing game I hope to see more from it soon
10/10
Such a shame - when it works it’s really good fun (especially ref mode), but it’s so buggy as to be basically unplayable currently

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback. We’ve just published version 1.12.6, with several improvements and a strong focus on stabilising Referee Mode.

We know that mode can be really fun, so we’ve been working hard on fixing the issues that had slipped through. If you try it again, we hope it already feels much better.

reviews.review_title: Wanted to like it but it needs work
reviews.review_description: There are things that have great potential and the option to play in many modes and views is what will make this great. The problem is actually playing a game of footy! Basic footy controls even in the Vr mode is awful and not simple even on novice. To have a pro mode like the old fifas or standard in VR mode would make me play this over and over but its just not enjoyablee. The dribble mode annoying as sprint makes you pause then run. Please make a standard footy game to play in Vr with pro mode and a management mode that is fun to watch and play in VR! So much potential but get one part great..... The football.

developer_response.body:
Hi, thanks for taking the time to share your feedback.

We understand that controls can feel different, especially if you’re coming from traditional football games. Motion Soccer uses a more immersive VR-focused approach and it can take a bit of time to adapt.

If you’re looking for a more arcade-style experience, try Classic control mode, Easy difficulty (which reduces stamina impact), or disable stamina in Chaos Lab.

We’re improving gameplay and animations with every update, and we appreciate you giving it a try.

reviews.review_description: Great game overall 😁, would be nice if the controls were improved for stuff like slide tackle, through ball, lob pass etc. not sure if it already exists but gamepad controller support might be good and a spectator view with a seat in the stadium

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for the 5⭐ review, I really appreciate it! 😁

Most of the things you mention are actually already in the game, although they can be a bit easy to miss at first:

• Slide tackles are automatic: if you don’t have the ball and press the trigger, your player will slide if the ball is on the ground, or jump if the ball is in the air.  
• Through balls and lob passes are automatic with the Standard control and auto-aim. You can also do them manually using the laser aiming option, or fully manual if you use the Expert control mode.  
• Gamepad support is already available.  
• Spectator view is also possible: choose the Coach view and then move to one of the spectator spots in the stands.

That said, your comment is still very helpful because it shows we probably need to explain these mechanics better in the tutorials or UI. Thanks again for playing Motion Soccer! ⚽

reviews.review_description: I downloaded the free version yesterday and it is so good I already want the pro version I’ve played go 3 whole battery lives already 10/10
It keeps flashing on and off

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the report. This usually happens when the voice command support did not download correctly.

You can normally fix it by going into Options and disabling the microphone voice instructions feature, or simply by reinstalling the game.

reviews.review_description: I don’t know if it’s just my game but sometimes my player moves the opposite way I go, this really messed up the game for me so I hope there’s a solution or something.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback, and sorry you ran into this. We’re not completely sure what’s causing it yet, because we still need a few more details, like which control method you’re using, which view/camera you were playing with, and exactly when this happens.

If you want to help us look into it, please email us at support@ivanovichgames.com, contact us through the official Discord, or reply here with more information. It would really help us improve the experience.

In any case, we’re here if you need anything. And if we manage to sort it out, we’d really appreciate it if you considered updating your review.

reviews.review_description: It a good game but when I try to switch to automatic movement expert mode it doesn’t work it almost feels like I have stick. Drift

developer_response.body:
In Expert Mode, movement becomes automatic towards the ball when you have possession and you are not pressing the joystick.

So when you say it does not work, what exactly do you mean? If the joystick is being detected as pressed or slightly tilted because of stick drift, the game will prioritize manual movement instead of automatic movement. In that case, it can definitely feel like the automatic system is not working, when the real issue is that the controller is constantly sending input.

So from what you describe, it really sounds more like stick drift than a bug in the game.

If you want, you can contact us at support@ivanovichgames.com or join our Discord and we can help you check it step by step.

reviews.review_description: The first game went well but whenever I try to swap teams or play again my game lags and then crashes I don’t know how to fix it, I deleted half my games trying to fix it, it just doesn’t want to work

developer_response.body:
Hi again, just a quick follow-up.

Since your review, Motion Soccer PRO has received 200+ updates, fixing stability issues like the one you reported and adding major new features such as Referee Mode, Season Mode and Multiplayer, along with many performance and usability improvements.

If you ever feel like giving the game another try in its current state, we’d genuinely love to hear your updated experience — and if it’s improved for you, updating the review really helps a small indie team like ours.

Thanks again for taking the time to report the issue back then.

reviews.review_description: Fun game
I started playing this game and it’s really good actually. I have been playing a lot but there is a few bugs with the ball stopping while your dribbling but besides that I have no complaints

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for the kind words and for pointing that out.

The ball stopping during dribbling was a known issue at the time of your review, and it has since been fully fixed in later updates. A lot of work went into improving ball physics and movement consistency.

If you ever feel like checking the game again in its current state, we’d genuinely appreciate your updated impression — and if that issue no longer affects your experience, updating the review is always appreciated.

Thanks again for playing and for the constructive feedback.

reviews.review_description: Really good game
recommend

Wow, I came into this game with low expectations and was blown away. There are so many modes and it seems they came in the recent update. No other sports title in vr has this many singleplayer modes, allowing you to compete in a full season (even mid season cups) and truly do it well like this.

Then I found out you can play as a ref!!! This is what I’ve always wanted in a videogame so thank you so much . You can even play more manager and player coach role which is actually awesome by itself.  

My only two wishes,would be allowing multiplayer for these modes and in general, expanding on the ref mode making it like the normal season mode with press conferences even, ha!

developer_response.body:
We’re really happy to hear you’re enjoying the different modes, especially the referee, manager, and season experiences. We do have plans to expand the referee mode, bring multiplayer, and add many more surprises along the way.

Thanks again for the support and for sharing your ideas — they help shape the future of the game.

reviews.review_description: Good ggma
This game is better then all the ones i ever played
Great game played all day!!
jhggh
nice game

First it’s football, but wow this game is awesome, if this is still in early development, wow, need a league and campaign and challenges but other than that it’s pretty cool well done devs FOOTBALL though 🤭

I love this game. I've played every famous soccer game in every video game, but this one is the best of them all. It's a shame people don't understand it because it's a very deep game. You can set it to play like a normal soccer game in a video game, but once you understand all the options and learn to play in first-person, everything feels like a real soccer game. I feel like I'm on the field. There are some errors in the Brazilian translation, and this leaves some people confused because this game has so many options. I would really like to help with the translation, as I'm Brazilian.
Congratulations on the best soccer game ever created in all of video games.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your amazing message and kind words! 🙌
We're thrilled to hear you're enjoying the game at that level — that's exactly what we were aiming for. ⚽💥

If you'd like to help us with the Brazilian translation, we’d truly appreciate it! You can reach us at support@ivanovichgames.com and we’ll guide you from there.

Thanks again for your support — it means a lot coming from someone who really understands the depth of the game. 🙏

reviews.review_description: So recently I’ve been loving playing this game until I couldn’t stop noticing some strange things, so first of all the game is really good and it is an insanely fun game to play. But then I realised that every time a through ball is played the defender sometimes stop? And some times the defending is just terrible to the point where they can’t make a proper 1-2 pass so also  check that out. And lastly if it isn’t rigged then please fix the game, because it is quite annoying how they can’t make passes. Oh and also forgot one thing, when using expert control can you please fix the dribbling? Because sometimes when dribbling the ball just stops or just randomly goes through your foot. But the rest of the game is good.

developer_response.body:
Hi again, just a quick follow-up.

The issues you described around dribbling, ball contact and inconsistent defender behavior were real at the time, and they’ve since been significantly improved through later updates focused on ball physics, player–ball interaction and AI decision-making.

Beyond that, the game has continued to evolve with new modes like Referee Mode and Season Mode, along with ongoing improvements to goalkeepers, controls, AI passing logic and overall stability.

If you ever feel like revisiting the game in its current state, we’d genuinely love to hear your updated impression — and if your experience feels better now, updating the review would be very welcome.

Thanks again for the detailed feedback and for supporting the game.

reviews.review_description: this game teaches u how to play football and i even had to get the pro it was this good w game next update should have trigger to change foot position to shoot, besides that this is a very good game
Didn’t meet my expectations

developer_response.body:
Hi, thanks for giving Motion Soccer PRO a try.

Just to add some context: the game is evolving very quickly, with frequent updates and major additions over the last months, including new modes and a lot of tuning based directly on player feedback.

If you ever feel like giving it another try in its current state, we’d genuinely appreciate hearing what works better for you now — and if your experience changes, updating the review really helps us as an indie team.

Thanks again for checking it out.

reviews.review_description: With recent updates I find it harder to possess the goalie and when playing expert mode I can kick all I see is his hands from the previous versions this wasn’t a issue other than that I do enjoy playing the game

developer_response.body:
Hi again, just a quick follow-up.

The goalkeeper control issues you described have since been fixed, and we’ve added more goalkeeper possession options in the settings, so players can fully customize when and how they want to control the keeper, including disabling automatic possession if preferred.

Beyond that, the game has continued to evolve with new additions like Referee Mode, Season Mode, and ongoing improvements to goalkeepers, controls, and overall stability.

If you ever feel like revisiting the game in its current state, we’d really appreciate your updated impression — and if it works better for you now, updating the review would be very welcome.

Thanks again for the feedback and for enjoying the game.

reviews.review_description: This game is amazing
Career when?

reviews.review_title: A MUST HAVE IF YOU LIKE FOOTBALL!!!
reviews.review_description: This game is SO much fun, even though it’s still in early access it’s still SO good. There’s a good bit of variety with the teams and you can also CUSTOMISE you’re team to you’re liking! And the developers take in ALL and yes I mean  ALL of you’re feedback and suggestions, Aslong as you’re in the discord of course

We’re so glad you’re having fun — and yep, we’re reading all the feedback on Discord and improving the game every day.
See you on the field! 🔥

reviews.review_description:
great idea, constant updates needed, but a winner- that’s for sure:) 
keep it up guys.

reviews.review_title: Good game for the price tag
reviews.review_description:
My previous review left this game at a 2/5, because of the bugs that ruined the experience, but 2 months later the bugs have been fixed and I can solidly put this game at a 4/5.

The VRFS controls have been improved, and no longer have your foot go through the ball. The AI has been improved on through balls, and gives a more fluid motion when playing balls.

This game is certainly the best paid soccer game on the Meta Quest as it stands.

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for coming back and updating your review — we really appreciate you giving the game another chance.

Since then, the game has continued to evolve with new additions like Season Mode and Referee Mode, along with further improvements to controls, AI behavior, goalkeepers and overall stability.

It’s great to see that the fixes to ball interaction, through balls and AI movement made a real difference for you — that’s exactly the direction we’ve been pushing.

Thanks again for the fair and detailed feedback and for supporting the game.

reviews.review_description:
I love the table top view absolutely brilliant idea. All other soccer games on VR you have to use your hands as feet, it's just awful for me and makes me dizzy but this game gives you the option to play like a video game, much like fifa but with a great subbuteo view. 
The commentators are dull as hell and hopefully something that can be developed but it doesn't put me off in ib cricket and doesn't in this. 
Keepers are shocking at times and corners are bloody hard to get right can't seem to get height but I think that's me being crap.
One annoying thing is having to unlock kits and to change player names, this is frustrating especially if the kit you want to customise your favourite team requires you to get to expert level. I'm a more i want it now sort of guy so if the developers can create a cheat code to unlock things without all the play that'll be great and would make the whole experience more enjoyable for me personally. 

Thank you to the developers, keep improving it please. 

Jon
This game is amazing and it has career mode witch even makes it better

When I dribble with the expert mode and joystick mode my feet just lags behind the ball ￼and I can’t dribble and sometimes it doesn’t even detect when I touch the ball.
If you guys fixed this it would be a very good game so please fix it

developer_response.body:
Hi again, just a quick follow-up.

The dribbling issues you described in Expert + joystick mode were real at the time and have since been fixed through later updates, with improvements to ball contact detection and player–ball synchronization.

Expert mode in particular has received a lot of tuning to make dribbling feel more responsive and consistent, without that “lagging behind the ball” sensation.

If you ever feel like trying the game again in its current state, we’d really appreciate your updated impression — and if it feels better for you now, updating the review would be very welcome.

Thanks again for reporting it; feedback like this helped us improve the mode.

reviews.review_description:
Absolutely surprised by this game and is the best VR football game I have played by far. It feels like football, the game has charm, banners like This is VR reminded me of the underrated football game This is Football. Names like London Red which can be edited brought a smile to my face thinking about PES. Even the commentary which sounds like they are commentating from an echoey bathroom like football games from the 90's is charming and amusing and I think I recognise one of the voices from Touring Karts which I enjoyed.

There are so many ways to play. First or Third person, a tactical mode with the field miniaturised in front of you, if you remember PES on the Wii where you would point the controller and drag the players into position it is like that.  

I am not into hands as feet hence why I did not get on with Premier League VR, VRFS or Football Nation VR  and play in the more traditional way with analog sticks which can also played in the different view points. The controls are excellent. For example while in first person taking a corner you can drag a player short by pointing at them so you can take the corner short or instead possess them and come short then possess the corner taker. So many options and things like throw ins where you can take it yourself like many things in the game bring a smile to my face.  

The Quest version while does the job admirably in visual terms I played the Steam demo and that looks better in terms of things going on in the crowd of the stadium to bring a bit more atmosphere. That version went straight on the wishlist waiting for a sale as it is a fair bit more expensive. I shall thoroughly enjoy the excellent Quest version.

I do hope Ivanovich Games will bring some of their games to PSVR2 including Motion Soccer PRO as they brought quite a few to PSVR. Throw a bit headset rumble in when heading the ball and some great advance haptics when saving a shot would be great.

I shall very much look forward to the updates.

Absolutely fantastic game for the Quest 3! I was blown away by how much fun it is. The developers did an outstanding job—pure genius! The controls are incredibly intuitive, making every moment a joy. Switching between three views and control methods is truly excellent. This is the ultimate soccer experience I’ve been dreaming of on Quest! 🚀⚽🔥
This is much better than the normal version this much better and less more limited! Also for the price tag its amazing!

Words can’t comprehend how good of a game this is, you just have to try it yourself. From the modes to the gameplay, you are getting the best experience you can achieve. You can upgrade your players, change your plays, and score from set pieces! This game is definitely a contender against the game VRFS, but only time will tell. A must have as those who have a vr but definitely for those who are looking for the best VR Soccer experience

The game has lots of potential however it is a bit buggy in terms of player movement and goalkeeping, as well as switching player overriding Goalkeeper dives so it messes up.

For this game to compete with the Premier League game and VRFS, it needs better movement and ball physics as well as fixing the janky controls

developer_response.body:
Hi again, just a quick follow-up.

Since your review, Motion Soccer PRO has gone through a large number of updates focused specifically on player movement, goalkeeper behavior, player switching logic and ball physics, which were exactly the areas you pointed out.

The way player switching interacts with goalkeeper actions has been refined, and overall movement has been made more predictable and consistent to avoid the kind of interruptions you described.

If you ever feel like giving the game another try in its current state, we’d genuinely value your updated impression — and if your experience improves, updating the review would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks again for the constructive feedback; it helped us prioritize the right things.

reviews.review_description:
I thought that having a football game would never happen unless we got official feet tracking in VR, but the use of the controllers in this game to dribble is great. I am getting leg straps to see if that would work for me since that would actually allow me to air dribble if I get the height adjustment right. 

There are a ton of options in the settings of this game, allowing for automatic movement, automatic changing of players, how passing works, and whether you want to play first person, third person, or tabletop view. I’m excited by the possibility of mixed reality tabletop since it’s listed as part of the plan. 

My preferred way to play is first person, move via joystick, and manually dribbling. You can trap the ball, head the ball, do a heel flick, and other moves if you have the space. I’m trying to get a rainbow flick done, but haven’t accomplished it yet. 

Passing manually works well, and the AI teammates respond well enough for early access. I recommend always being the ball handler, and when you make a pass you can look at your teammate and control them with a press of the trigger. The time slows down while you warp to that player, so it’s not disorienting. 

The devs are looking into feet tracking already, and for early access it’s already fun to play. I can’t wait to see how well they fine tune  the physics, movement, and AI by launch.
This game is honestly one of the best VR games out there, it has so many modes in the game and multiple ways to play! Definitely worth the money, money well spent!
Lovely game. It would be lovely  if players  could head the ball .and there is updates now and again. Brilliant  game  i would love to give this football game ten stars 🌟 if I could

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your kind words and support! We're thrilled you're enjoying the game. 😊 We're happy to share that both players (in any view) and the AI can perform headers—sometimes even scoring amazing goals! ⚽️ Stay tuned for more updates, and thank you again for your feedback! 🌟

reviews.review_description:
Updates So Often
Gameplay is good
